children.name: Count Rinaldo or Rainaldo D' Aquino

zh.Aquino, Terra di Lavoro, Italy: 義大利特拉 迪 拉沃洛艾奎諾
zh.lifeSummary: Conte Landone IV d'Aquino是Conte Landone III d'Aquino和Ignota的兒子，於1080年出生在義大利特拉 迪 拉沃洛艾奎諾。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.Aquino, Terra di Lavoro, Italy: 意大利特拉 迪 拉沃洛艾奎諾

nameMeanings.name: d'Aquino
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Nicola, Stefano, Gaetano, Margherita, Nunzio, Sal, Salvatore.  Spanish Alba, Jose Alejandro, Pedro, Socorro.

Italian: habitational name for someone from a place called Aquino, of which there are several examples in Italy, but in particular the one in Lazio, birthplace of Saint Thomas Aquinas (see   Aquino  ).

variants.name: Aquino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Antonio, Pedro, Juan, Manuel, Mario, Ana, Jesus, Jorge, Josefina, Julio, Luis.  Portuguese Paulo, Catarina, Joao.  Italian Romeo, Luigi, Salvatore, Clemente, Leonardo.

Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: from a Christian personal name bestowed in honor of Saint Thomas Aquinas (1225–74; Tommaso d'Aquino in Italian, Tomás de Aquino in Spanish), an Italian Dominican friar and Doctor of the Church. This surname is also common in the Philippines. See also 2 below, compare De Aquino .
Italian: habitational name from a place called Aquino (see   D'Aquino  ), the place where the theologian (see 1 above) was born.

nameMeanings.name: Conte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Silvio, Silverio, Giuseppe, Sal, Alfonso, Biagio, Carlo, Emilio, Fernando, Gerardo.

Italian: from the title of rank conte ‘count’, from Latin comes, genitive comitis ‘companion’. Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .
Spanish: variant of   Conde   ‘count’ (see also 1 above).

variants.name: Conti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Gino, Carlo, Domenic, Rocco, Sal, Aldo, Carmela, Enrico, Guido.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Conte  .

variants.name: Conde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Jorge, Ruben, Angel, Cesar, Fernando.  Portuguese Goncalo, Manoel.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from the title of rank conde ‘count’, a derivative of Latin comes, comitis ‘companion’. See Conte .
English (Shropshire): variant of Cundy , a cognate of 3 below, or Cound, a habitational name from Cound in Shropshire.

variants.name: Contessa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmela, Gaspare, Rocco, Santo.
Italian:
from contessa ‘countess’, feminine form of Conte .

variants.name: Contino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Amato, Sal, Vito, Anella, Carmelo, Carmine, Domiano, Luigi, Pasquale, Salvator, Salvatore, Vincenza.
Italian: from a diminutive of conte ‘count’ (see Conte ).

variants.name: Lo Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Vito, Angelo, Biagio, Carmine, Francesco, Gino, Manlio, Pasquale, Vincenza.
Italian (southern; also Loconte): variant of Conte , with the addition of the definite article lo.

variants.name: Del Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dario, Rocco, Sal, Americo, Claudio, Domenic, Estanislao, Luciano, Mario, Nevio.
Italian (mainly Tuscany): literally ‘of or belonging to the count’(see   Conte  ), hence a name denoting the servant or possibly theson of a count or a patronymic from the nickname.

nameMeanings.name: Landone
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly U.S.: transferred use of the surname, in origin a local name from any of various places in England called Langdon, from Old English lang ‘long’ + dūn ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: Conte Landone IV d'Aquino was born in 1080, in Aquino, Terra di Lavoro, Italy as the son of Conte Landone III d'Aquino and Ignota. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Conte Landone IV d'Aquino est né en 1080, à Aquino, Terra di Lavoro, Italie, fils de Conte Landone III d'Aquino et Ignota. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Conte Landone IV d'Aquino wurde 1080 in Aquino, Terra di Lavoro, Italien, als Sohn von Conte Landone III d'Aquino und Ignota geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Conte Landone IV d'Aquino è nato il nel 1080 nel Aquino, Terra di Lavoro, Italia come figlio di Conte Landone III d'Aquino e Ignota. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Aquino, Terra di Lavoro, Italy: イタリアテラ ジー ラボロアクイノ
ja.lifeSummary: Conte Landone IV d'Aquinoは1080年にイタリアテラ ジー ラボロアクイノでConte Landone III d'AquinoとIgnotaの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Conte Landone IV d'Aquino(은)는 1080년 이탈리아 터라 디 라보로 아키노에서 Conte Landone III d'Aquino(와)과 Ignota의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Conte Landone IV d'Aquino nasceu em 1080, em Aquino, Terra di Lavoro, Itália filho de Conte Landone III d'Aquino e Ignota. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Conte Landone IV d'Aquino родился в 1080 г. в местности акуино, тирра ди лаворо, Италия. Его родителей звали Conte Landone III d'Aquino и Ignota. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Conte Landone IV d'Aquino nació en 1080, en Aquino, Terra di Lavoro, Italia como hijo de Conte Landone III d'Aquino y Ignota. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
